titleStmt.title: SII 9.476: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 476. (A.R. No. 423 of 1928.) ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF VENKATARAMANA AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 1428, Prabhava, Kārtika, śu. 15, Śa[nivāra], corresponding to A.D. 1506 October 31, Saturday; Prabhava is a mistake for Kshaya which was Śaka 1428 expired. The inscription refers itself to the reign of Vīra-Narasiṅgadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records that Basavarasa-Voḍeya raised, as subscription from the villagers of Baḍākere in Kandāura, some money and made a grant of it to the temple of the god Tirumaladēva for burning a perpetual lamp before the god out of the interest on the sum. It also states that some other persons made a grant of gadyāṇas 25, being the annual income of a garden, for the service of the same god; it further registers a gift made by the Halaru of Baḍākere of varāhas 7 got as interest from the Brāhmans of the place on a certain sum outstanding on them as a loan, for the service of the god.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrī gaṇādhipatayē nama[ḥ .*] namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdracāmaracāravē . trayilō
kyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē . svasti śrī jayādbhudayaśālivāhanaśakavaru
ṣa 1427 saṁda yaṁṭaneya prabhavasaṁvatsarada kārttika śu 15 śa lū śrīma(ṁ)nmahārājādhi
rāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīranarasiṁgadēvarāyamahārāyaru pṛthvī
rājyaṁgeyidu vijayanagariya siṁhāsanadalū cittayisi samastaduṣṭanigrahaśiṣṭaprati
pālakanāgi sakalavarnadharmmaśrimaṁgaḷanu pratipālisutiha kāladalu narasiṁgadēvarā
yamahārāyarige śatrukṣaya mitrōrjita ĀyurārōgyaAyiśvaryyaAbhiUradhiyā
gabēkeṁdu basavarasavoḍeyarū basuṟūra paḍuvakēriya tirumaladēvarige moserōga
ra nayivēdyake kaṁdāurada grāmada voḷage kuḷava kaḍidu dhāreyaneṟadu barasi koṭa dha
rmmaśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare Aramaneya bhaṁḍārastaḷada kaṁdāurada grāmada voḷagaṇa
baḍākereyalu tirumaladēvarige mosarōgara nayivēdyake kuḷava kaḍidu dhāreyaneṟadu koṭadu
kalu 2 kaṁ . ghaṭi varaha ga 5 ¥ 2 tirumaladēvarige naṁdādīptigū dinamānā vecaku basavarasavoḍe
yaru dhārāpūrvakavāgi dhāreyaneṟadu koṭadu tirumaladēvarige mūḍalāgi hōda sāliga
ra kēriya maga garavāragaḷanu A mūḍabāgila suṁkavanū Ācaṁdrārkkavāgi tirumaladēvari
ge naṁdādīptiṁgū dinamānavecavanū naḍasuvaṁtāgi dhāreyaneṟadu koṭaṁthā dharmmaśāśanake śu
bhamastu basavarasavoḍeyara voppa bārakūra karaṇika mallarasara barha . sudattaṁ vā paradattaṁ vā vō ha
rētu vasuṁdhara śaṣṭirvaruśasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ . dānapālanayōrmmadhyē dā
nāśrēyōnrupālanaṁ dānātu svarggamavāpnōti pālanādacutaṁ padaṁ . paḍuvakēryya ti
rumaladēvālyasthanada sōmana dharmmapārupatyavanū nallāḷacakravartti ĀnaṁtaĀ
yyagaḷa saṁtānapāreṁpareyāgi paḍuvakēryya halaru seṭikāṟarū sahitavāgi naḍasū
dakke sahāyavādavaru vāraṇāsiyali sāvira kavileyanu brā(ṁ)hmarige koṭṭu sukru
tavanū paḍeyaluḷavaru Ā dhar(ṁ)mma naḍasūdake vighnabaṁdavage vāraṇāsiya
lu sāvira kavileyanu koṁda dōśake hōgaluḷavaru śubhamastu maṁgaḷa mahā
śrī śrī śrī . tirumaladēvarige naṁdādīptige Āṁṇiseṭiya māraseṭi biṭa tōṭada vi
vara mūḍakēriya hosakēriya voḷage kupseṭṭiya yīsaraseṭṭiya magaḷa makkaḷu dugseṭi
ya nāraṇaseṭiya maga be(ṁ)mmaseṭi taṁgakaseṭitiya maga be(ṁ)mmabhaṁḍāri lakuma
seṭitiya magaḷu māṁdammanu Ī mūvarū tāyamakaḷa mūlada tōṭavanu mādaseṭi Ādi
yāgi māḍikoṁḍa tōṭake koṭa vāra ga 25 Ā tōṭada gēṇiyanu tirumaladēvarige naṁdādī
ptige Ācaṁdrārkkavāgi naḍasūva dharmma tirumaladēvara paḍuvaṇa Aśvatada kaṭeya dharmma
ke karadēvabaḷiya nāraṇamodalanū māḍidu basavaṁtana naḍigeya hittilu
tōṭadalū gēṇi hoṁnū varaha ga 2 Ī vārahanige Ā kaṭege mettisi dha
rmmada Udakavanū Irisisi dīvaḷige habba muṁtāgi dīptiyanū hottisi Ā dha
rmmava Ācaṁdrarkkavāgi naḍasuvaru tirumaladēvara naṁdādīptige paḍuvakēryya ha
larū koṭaru kaṁdāvarada baḍākareya brāṁhara mēle mayisālada baḍḍi varuṣa 1
ke naḍasuva varaha ga 7 Ēḷu varahananū Ācaṁdrarkavāgi neḍasi naṁdādīptige naḍasi
māḍida dharmma .
Digital edition of SII 9.476 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
